description_long: This object consists of a group of freestanding advertising boards arranged in a loose grid, each supported by angled legs. The boards vary in size and shape, with most being rectangular and a few square, and they display a variety of colorful printed advertisements, including logos, product names, and stylized graphics. The content appears to be static, with no visible digital or illuminated features, and the boards are constructed from what looks like wood or a similar rigid material, with printed paper or vinyl surfaces. The frames are simple and utilitarian, with thin borders and no decorative elements, and the boards are designed to be easily movable and placed on flat ground. The rear view reveals plain, unadorned backs, confirming that the advertisements are only on the front-facing surfaces. This collection is typical of temporary or semi-permanent advertising installations found in urban public spaces.
description: A group of freestanding advertising boards with colorful printed ads, supported by simple wooden frames and angled legs, designed for urban public spaces.
description_view_0: Front view showing a variety of colorful advertisements and logos on rectangular and square boards, all supported by angled legs.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the thin profile of the boards and the angled support legs, with minimal visibility of the advertisement content.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the plain, unadorned backs of the boards, with the support structures clearly visible and no advertisement content.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the staggered arrangement and angled legs, with only the edges of the boards and some side graphics visible.
attribute_frame_design: thin wooden frame with simple straight edges
attribute_advertisement_content: static printed posters for various brands and products
